State of California

Memorandum

To         : Advice File Sections 87400-87405                Date    : May 20, 1982

                                                              File No. M-82-094


From      : FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
              Bob Leidigh

Subject:    Legislative History and Interpretation of AB 1048, Ch. 66,
            Stats 1980 Which Enacted Sections 87400-87405


            Attached hereto are pertinent materials from our
      legislative file on AB 1048 which was Commission-sponsored
      legislation.  Additional materials are available on the
      bill file in our long-term storage facility.

      BL:plh
      Attachment

DATE TYPED: 2-28-80

Sponsor: Fair Political Practices Commission

Support: California State Employees Association BILL NUMBER: AB (1048)

Opposed: Association of California State AUTHOR: M. Waters
         Attorneys

Committee Votes:                    Senate Floor Vote:        AMENDED COPY: 2-25-80

[THIS IS TABLE: Committee votes table showing various committee members and their votes, appearing to show 2/3rds vote requirement]

PLACED ON FILE AS
A RESULT OF SENATE
RULE 28.8

Assembly Floor Vote: 65-8 p.7139 6/25/79

DIGEST

1
2    AB 1048 would prohibit any former state administrative official,
3    after termination of his/her employment of term of office, from
4    representing for compensation any other person (other than the
5    State of California) in connection with a judicial, quasi-
6    judicial or other proceeding in which a) the State of California
7    is a party or has a direct and substantial interest, and b) the
8    proceeding is one in which the former state administrative
9    official participated.
10   (For specifics of the bill see Comments).
11
12   FISCAL EFFECT:
13   Appropriation: No.  Fiscal Committee: Yes.  County: No.
14
15   The Fair Political Practices Commission anticipates insignificant
16   costs.
17
18   COMMENTS:
19   Under current law, the Political Reform Act does not provide
20   restrictions on the activities of officers and employees who
21   formerly served in the executive branch of state government
22   and who subsequently engage in post-government employment for
23   compensation.
24
25   Sponsors of AB 1048 feel that the interest of state government
26   and the public can best be protected by:
27
28   1.  Preventing the disclosure of use of matters revealed by
29       reason of a relationship having confidential aspects;
30
31   2.  reducing the chance that post-government employment con-
32       siderations will bias the conduct of government business;
33       and
34

36                                        - NEXT PAGE -

CENTRAL ANALYSIS                                    BUSINESS and Transportation Agent

SUBJECT
POLITICAL REFORM ACT: RESTRICTED
ACTIVITIES OF FORMER STATE EMPLOYEES

AUTHOR
MAXINE WATERS

BILL NUMBER
AB 1040

DATE
2/25/80


The latest amendment redefines the term "state administrative
official" for purposes of the bill to mean every member, officer,
employee, or consultant of a state administrative agency who, as a
part of his or her official responsibilities, engages in any judicial,
quasi-judicial, or other proceeding, rather than in any administrative
action, in other than a purely clerical, secretarial or ministerial
capacity.

In other words, this vastly broadens the scope of the bill because of the reference to the term "or other proceeding."

This only makes a bad bill worse.

Although our directed position is that of NEUTRAL, suggest
amendments, we continue to urge an OPPOSE position. We contacted the
author's office and attempted to suggest amendments, but to no avail.


[illegible]                    OFFICE OF INSURANCE              DATE MAR 5  1980


MAR 12 1980

Assemblywoman [illegible] Waters
Page Two                                                    April 24, 1979


IT IS VERY NARROWLY WRITTEN SO AS ONLY TO COVER A SPECIFIC

MATTER IN WHICH THE FORMER EMPLOYEE WAS INVOLVED WHILE ON THE

STATE PAYROLL.

     THE BILL IS SPONSORED BY THE FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES

COMMISSION.  IT HAS NO KNOWN OPPOSITION.


RMS:mc

State of California

Memorandum

To : Assembly[man] Vixin [illegible]                     Date : April 24, 1979


From : FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
        Robert M. Stern

Subject: STATEMENT ON AB 1048


AB 1048 PROHIBITS THE MISUSE OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION BY

FORMER STATE EXECUTIVE OFFICIALS.

THE BILL PREVENTS FORMER EXECUTIVE OFFICIALS FROM REPRESENTING

PRIVATE CLIENTS IN MATTERS IN WHICH THEY PARTICIPATED PERSONALLY

AND SUBSTANTIALLY WHILE ON THE PUBLIC PAYROLL.

THE FORMER OFFICIALS WOULD BE BARRED FROM REPRESENTING FOR

COMPENSATION PRIVATE CLIENTS IN SUCH MATTERS AS JUDICIAL CASES,

ADMINISTRATIVE PROCEDURES OR CONTRACTUAL RELATIONSHIPS, IF

DURING THEIR GOVERNMENT SERVICE, THEY WERE PERSONALLY AND SUB-

STANTIALLY INVOLVED IN THOSE SAME MATTERS.

THE BILL IS NOT A BROAD REVOLVING DOOR BILL. IT DOES NOT

PROHIBIT FORMER STATE EMPLOYEES FROM ATTEMPTING TO INFLUENCE THEIR

FORMER AGENCY.

[illegible text at bottom right]

SENATE DEMOCRATIC CAUCUS [Bill No. AB ] Amended: 2-25-80
SENATOR OMAR L. RAINS, Chairman
Author: Maxine Waters (D)

Subject: Conflict of Interest; Restricted Activities of Former State
Officers and Employees

Finance Committee:
Be placed on Second Reading File pursuant to Senate Rule 28.R.

Policy Committee: Governmental Organization

Ayes ( 5 ) Alquist, Cusanovich, Foran, Greene, Dills

Noes ( 0 )

Assembly Floor Vote: 65 AYES; 0 NOES.

Summary of Legislation:
Under existing law, the Political Reform Act does not provide for restrictions on the activities of officers and employees who formerly served in the executive branch of state government, but who subsequently engage in post-government employment for corporations.
This bill provides for such restrictions. It prohibits representation, as specified, by a former agency official of a post-government employment client in a judicial, quasi-judicial or other proceeding in which the State of California is a party or has a direct and substantial interest and the former agency official had participated. This bill further prohibits the former agency official from aiding, assisting or advising anyone else in preparing such a client in such a matter. If the official himself would be prohibited from serving in a representative capacity.

This bill provides exceptions to the regulatory provisions of the article, as specified. This bill empowers the indor, provide officer or other person in charge of a pending proceeding, upon the petition of an interested person and after notice and opportunity for a hearing, to exclude anyone found to be in violation of the proposed article from further participation in the pending proceeding.

Finally, this bill imposes the requirements of the proposed article only prospectively, so that only persons leaving state office or employment after the effective date of the article would be covered thereby.

Fiscal Effect:
No added state cost.

Proponents:
Fair Political Practices Commission (sponsor)
California State Employees Association

Opponents:

Arguments in Support:
Proponents state that the interest of state government and the public can best be protected by (1) preventing the disclosure or use of matters revealed by reason of a relationship having confidential aspects; (2) reducing the chance that post-government employment considerations will bias the conduct of government business; and (3) imposing limitations on the opportunity for undue advantage by reason of familiarity with agency practice and procedure, and personal relationships with agency personnel.

Arguments in Opposition:

---

LFD-61 2-25-80

Assembly Bill No. 1048

CHAPTER 66

An act to add Article 4 (commencing with Section 87400) to Chapter 7 of Title 9 of the Government Code, relating to political reform.

[Approved by Governor April 21, 1980. Filed with
Secretary of State April 21, 1980.]

LEGISLATIVE COUNSEL'S DIGEST

AB 1048, M. Waters. Conflict of interest-restricted activities of former state officers and employees.

Under existing law, the Political Reform Act does not provide for restrictions on the activities of officers and employees who formerly served in the executive branch of state government but who subsequently engaged in post-government employment for compensation.

This bill would provide for such restrictions. It would prohibit representation, as specified, by a former agency official of a post-government employment client in a judicial, quasi-judicial, or other proceeding, in which the State of California is a party or has a direct and substantial interest, and the former agency official had participated. This bill would further prohibit the former agency official from aiding, assisting, or advising anyone else in representing such a client in such a matter if the official himself would be prohibited from serving in a representative capacity.

This bill would provide exceptions to the regulatory provisions of the article, as specified.

This bill would empower the judge, presiding officer or other person in charge of a pending proceeding, upon the petition of an interested person and after notice and opportunity for a hearing, to exclude anyone found to be in violation of the proposed article from further participation in the pending proceeding.

Finally, this bill would impose the requirements of the proposed article only prospectively, so that only persons leaving state office or employment after the effective date of the article would be covered thereby.

The people of the State of California do enact as follows:

SECTION 1. Article 4 (commencing with Section 87400) is added to Chapter 7 of Title 9 of the Government Code, to read:

Article 4. Disqualification of Former Officers and Employees

87400. Unless the contrary is stated or clearly appears from the context, the definitions set forth in this section shall govern the

92 50

Ch. 66 — 2 —

interpretation of this article.
(a) "State administrative agency" means every state office,
department, division, bureau, board and commission, but does not
include the Legislature, the courts or any agency in the judicial
branch of government.
(b) "State administrative official" means every member, officer,
employee or consultant of a state administrative agency who as part
of his or her official responsibilities engages in any judicial,
quasi-judicial or other proceeding in other than a purely clerical,
secretarial or ministerial capacity.
(c) "Judicial, quasi-judicial or other proceeding" means any
proceeding, application, request for a ruling or other determination,
contract, claim, controversy, investigation, charge, accusation, arrest
or other particular matter involving a specific party or parties in any
court or state administrative agency, including but not limited to any
proceeding governed by Chapter 5 (commencing with Section
11500) of Division 3 of Title 2 of the Government Code.
(d) "Participated" means to have taken part personally and
substantially through decision, approval, disapproval, formal written
recommendation, rendering advice on a substantial basis,
investigation or use of confidential information as an officer or
employee, but excluding approval, disapproval or rendering of legal
advisory opinions to departmental or agency staff which do not
involve a specific party or parties.
87401. No former state administrative official, after the
termination of his or her employment or term of office, shall for
compensation act as agent or attorney for, or otherwise represent,
any other person (other than the State of California) before any
court or state administrative agency or any officer or employee
thereof:
(a) By making any formal or informal appearance, or by making
any oral or written communication with the intent to influence, in
connection with any judicial, quasi-judicial or other proceeding if;
(b) The State of California is a party or has a direct and substantial
interest; and
(c) If the proceeding is one in which the former state
administrative official participated.
87402. No former state administrative official, after the
termination of his or her employment or term of office shall for
compensation aid, advise, counsel, consult or assist in representing
any other person (except the State of California) in any proceeding
in which the official would be prohibited from appearing under
Section 87401.
87403. The prohibitions contained in Sections 87401 and 87402
shall not apply:
(a) To prevent a former state administrative official from making
or providing a statement, which is based on the former state
administrative official's own special knowledge in the particular area

92 70

—3—
Ch. 66

that is the subject of the statement, provided that no compensation
is thereby received other than that regularly provided for by law or
regulation for witnesses; or

(b) To communications made solely for the purpose of furnishing
information by a former state administrative official if the court or
state administrative agency to which the communication is directed
makes findings in writing that:

(1) The former state administrative official has outstanding and
otherwise unavailable qualifications;

(2) The former state administrative official is acting with respect
to a particular matter which requires such qualifications; and

(3) The public interest would be served by the participation of the
former state administrative official; or

(c) With respect to appearances or communications in a
proceeding in which a court or state administrative agency has issued
a final order, decree, decision or judgment but has retained
jurisdiction if the state administrative agency of former employment
gives consent by determining that:

(1) At least five years have elapsed since the termination of the
former state administrative official's employment or term of office;
and

(2) The public interest would not be harmed.

§ 8704. Upon the petition of any interested person or party, the
court or the presiding or other officer, including but not limited to
a hearing officer serving pursuant to Section 11512 of the
Government Code in any judicial, quasi-judicial or other
proceeding, including including but not limited to any proceeding pursuant to
Chapter 5 (commencing with Section 11500) of Part 1 of Division 3
of Title 2 of the Government Code may, after notice and an
opportunity for a hearing, exclude any person found to be in
violation of this article from further participation, or from assisting
or counseling any other participant, in the proceeding then pending
before such court or presiding or other officer.

§ 8705. The requirements imposed by this article shall not apply
to any person who left government service prior to the effective date
of this article except that any such person who returns to
government service on or after the effective date of this article shall
thereafter be covered thereby.

SEC. 2. The Legislature finds and declares that the provisions of
this act further the purposes of the Political Reform Act of 1974
within the meaning of subdivision (a) of Section 81012 of the
Government Code.

O

92 100

sections.question_synthetic: This document provides legislative history and analysis of AB 1048, which enacted restrictions on former state administrative officials' post-government employment activities, specifically prohibiting them from representing private clients in matters where they previously participated.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is not a traditional advice letter with Q/A sections. It is primarily legislative documentation including bill digest, committee analysis, legislative counsel's digest, and the full text of enacted legislation. No specific legal question or FPPC advice conclusion provided. Confidence reduced due to unusual document structure for FPPC advice letter file.

embedding.summary: This is a 1982 FPPC memorandum documenting the legislative history and interpretation of AB 1048 (Chapter 66, Stats 1980), Commission-sponsored legislation that added sections 87400-87405 to the Government Code, restricting post-government employment activities of former state administrative officials.
